summary: DHH announces he's rebuilt his blog (Loud Thinking) from scratch using Rails instead of using an existing blogging package. He frames the decision as deliberately inefficient but valuable as a learning experience for setting up a small Rails app with a modern stack. He notes that he hasn't implemented comments yet and suggests he prefers the resulting quiet.
key_points: Chose to build a custom blog with Rails rather than use existing blogging software, prioritizing learning over productivity
Used a then-modern stack of Ubuntu Feisty, nginx, Mongrel, and SQLite3 for the setup
Frames the absence of comments as an accidental benefit, enjoying the tranquility of a comment-free blog
money_quotes: Instead of going with one of the million packages out there, I decided to eat some dog food and just roll my own.
Yes, yes, terribly inefficient from a productivity perspective, but I indulged myself with a learning experience.
I haven't bothered implementing comments for my little machine just yet. And I'm thinking that's actually a blessing in part disguise.
I think I'll be happy with the tranquillity for a while.
key_insight: Sometimes building your own tool from scratch, despite being less efficient, is worthwhile for the learning experience and the freedom to skip features you don't actually want.
